seoTitle: Connect Facebook Pages to GoHighLevel: Setup Guide
metaDescription: Link your Facebook Page to GoHighLevel to manage Messenger conversations, sync leads, and automate follow-ups from your CRM.
intro: Managing Facebook conversations and leads directly from GoHighLevel saves you from switching between platforms and lets you automate follow-ups more consistently. This tutorial covers authorizing your Meta account, syncing your page, and setting up workflows tied to Facebook Messenger.

steps.heading: Open integration settings
steps.body: Navigate to the section of GoHighLevel where third-party integrations, including Facebook, are managed.

steps.heading: Connect your Facebook Page
steps.body: Authorize your Meta account and connect the specific Facebook Page you want linked to GoHighLevel.

steps.heading: Configure permissions and sync
steps.body: Grant the required permissions so your page syncs correctly, allowing messages and leads to flow into your CRM.

steps.heading: Manage messages and leads from your CRM
steps.body: Use GoHighLevel to view and respond to Facebook Messenger conversations and leads directly from your dashboard.

steps.heading: Automate follow-ups with workflows
steps.body: Set up workflows that trigger campaigns and automated follow-ups based on Facebook lead activity.

faq.question: Do I need a Meta account to connect my Facebook Page?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial covers authorizing your Meta account as part of the connection process.

faq.question: Can I manage Facebook Messenger from GoHighLevel?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial covers managing messages and leads directly from your CRM once the page is connected.

faq.question: Can I automate responses to new Facebook leads?
faq.answer: Yes, automating lead follow-ups and triggering campaigns through workflows is a core part of the tutorial.

faq.question: What permissions does this integration require?
faq.answer: The tutorial covers granting the required page permissions needed to sync your Facebook Page with GoHighLevel.

faq.question: Who benefits most from this integration?
faq.answer: Agencies, marketers, business owners, social media managers, and sales teams are highlighted as the target audience.
